exemplar.title: To what extent is The Body Shopâs CSR measures making its supply chain more sustainable

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent is The Body Shopâs CSR measures making its supply chain more sustainable

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Explicitly defines sustainability and links it to CSR and the tripleâbottomâline
Consistently revisits the three pillars of sustainability in each analytical section
Connects theoretical concept to practice via refill scheme, net-zero ambition and ethical sourcing
weaknesses: Some descriptive passages (e.g., lengthy supplier-code quotation) drift away from the key concept
Analysis is strong but not fully pervasiveâoccasional sections lack explicit ties back to sustainability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands were assessed at band 4 (strong understanding with partial integration). The investigation consistently frames around sustainability and connects it to The Body Shopâs initiatives, but occasional descriptive digressions prevent seamless, throughout integration required for band 5. Thus a final score of 4/5 is justified.

strengths: Inclusion of corporate reports (The Body Shop Sustainability Report, Natura & Co data) and academic sources demonstrates breadth
Relevant quantitative evidence (plastic saved, COâ reductions, loyalty stats) enriches the investigation
weaknesses: Provided more than five supporting documents, violating the 3â5 range and lowering the criterion to band 1
Depth is unevenâseveral sources are used only for basic definitions or generic statements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the sources used are relevant and in-depth in places (band 3), the document count exceeds five, placing the work at band 1 for the ânumber of documentsâ strand. According to rubric rules, the highest band where both strandsâ requirements are met is band 1, so the final score is 1/4.

strengths: Purposeful selection of three recognised tools (Triple Bottom Line, CSR Matrix, Stakeholder Map)
Tools are appropriately linked to The Body Shopâs CSR and supply-chain issues
weaknesses: Application sometimes remains surface-level and descriptive rather than critically deep
Limited numerical or comparative analysis to reinforce theoretical insights
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Selection of tools meets band 4 (effective choice), but application is adequate rather than highly effective (band 3). The highest band where both strandsâ descriptors are satisfied is band 3, yielding a score of 3/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Integrates external survey data and quantitative emission reductions to support analysis
Structured discussion of benefits, limitations and stakeholder effects across tools
weaknesses: Data selection is adequate but not extensiveâsome claims lack quantitative support
Analysis occasionally drifts into description and lacks sustained integration between sections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both data selection and analysis quality were assessed at band 3 (âsome analysisâ and âadequate use of dataâ). The work does not meet the higher consistency and depth of band 4 or 5, so the final score is 3/5.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented throughout
Explicitly answers the research question with clear reference to quantitative and qualitative findings
weaknesses: Conclusion could be strengthened by deeper integration of sustainability theory
Lacks a final statement tying back to broader implications or next steps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion meets all requirements for band 3 (fully consistent with evidence and explicit answer to the research question) and does not fall short of any descriptor for that band, so it receives the maximum 3/3.

strengths: Logical structure with clear introduction, analytical sections and conclusion
Well-organized table of contents and appropriate headings guide the reader
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in header formatting could be improved
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Meets all descriptors for band 2 (appropriate structure throughout) without significant lapses, earning the full 2/2.

strengths: Includes all required presentation elements: research question, word count, citations, reference list, images, appendix
Formatting is generally clear and professional
weaknesses: Some footnote and citation formatting inconsistencies
Occasional proofreading errors (incomplete sentences) detract slightly
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are satisfied (band 2). Minor formatting inconsistencies do not drop it below the top band, so it receives 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure (introduction, separate analytical sections, conclusion) and includes a table of contents.  Headings and sub-headings guide the reader effectively, meeting expectations for an âappropriate structureâ, which is band 2.

Sources such as The Body Shop Sustainability Report, Natura & Co annual data, Statista brand metrics and Packaging Gateway are directly relevant and provide useful quantitative evidence (plastic saved, COâ reduced, customer loyalty).  Depth is adequate but uneven; several citations are used only for definitions or generic statements, limiting range and insight.  This aligns with ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the richer range expected for band 4.
All required elementsâresearch question, word count, citations, reference list, images, and appendix materialâare present.  Hence it satisfies band 2 for presentation.
The appendix/citation list contains well over five documents, exceeding the 3-5 range prescribed.  Under the rubric, providing more than five documents places the work in band 1 for this strand.

Each tool is applied to The Body Shop with clear if sometimes surface-level links: TBL analysis ties the refill scheme to the three pillars, the materiality matrix is used to discuss net-zero, and the stakeholder map focuses on suppliers.  Explanations of benefits and limitations show adequate application, yet sections remain descriptive and lack critical depth (e.g., limited numerical cost analysis).  Thus application is âadequateâ rather than fully effectiveâband 3.

Data such as tonnes of plastic saved, COâ reductions and customer loyalty percentages are incorporated, and the student quotes supplier-code clauses to illustrate social impact.  The selection is adequate but not extensive; some claims (cost efficiency, revenue impact) lack supporting figures.  Use of data informs discussion without being consistently rigorous, fitting band 3 for data selection and use.

The student offers clear, structured analysisâbenefits, limitations, stakeholder effectsâacross all three tools, directly addressing the research question.  Nonetheless, reasoning sometimes drifts into description, and integration between different analytical sections is limited, so the work sits at âsome analysisâ rather than âmostly effectiveâ or âthoroughâ, aligning with band 3.

The student explicitly answers the research question in the conclusion, stating that CSR measures have a âlarge extentâ effect and justifying this with prior findings.  This direct response fulfils band 3 requirements.

The student selects three recognised toolsâTriple Bottom Line, CSR (materiality) Matrix and Stakeholder Mapâeach suited to assessing CSR and supply-chain sustainability.  This variety demonstrates an effective, purposeful choice rather than a merely adequate or limited selection, placing it in band 4.

The conclusion synthesises qualitative insights with the quantitative evidence cited (plastic saved, emission cuts, loyalty stats) and repeats the judgment that CSR measures have a âlarge extentâ impact.  No claims contradict earlier data, so conclusions are consistent with evidence, earning the top band 3 for this criterion.

The IA keeps The Body Shop at the centre of discussion, using specific company initiatives (refill programme, emission data, Community Fair Trade suppliers) to demonstrate impact.  Analytical commentsâsuch as cost-savings from circular packaging and reputational gains from net-zeroâshow a strong, partially integrated link between firm context and the arguments.  Minor sections remain generic (definition paragraphs, stakeholder-map theory) so the connection, while strong, is not fully seamless, warranting band 4.

The student consistently frames the investigation around the key concept of sustainabilityâdefining it, linking it to CSR and the triple-bottom-line, and revisiting the three pillars in each analytical section.  References to the refill scheme, net-zero ambition and ethical sourcing clearly connect sustainability theory to practice.  However, the integration is not fully pervasive; some passages (e.g., lengthy supplier-code quotation) drift into descriptive detail without tying back to the concept.  This reflects strong but only partially integrated analysis, matching band 4 rather than the seamless, throughout integration expected for band 5.

annotations.comment: The extensive listing of supplier code clauses is descriptive but could be synthesized to highlight the most critical elements affecting sustainability.
Consolidate overlapping supplier regulations into thematic groups to strengthen strategic analysis and reduce descriptive bulk.
Integrating external survey data on consumer priorities and brand awareness provides credible evidence for the social dimension of sustainability.
The research question is clearly stated and focused, providing a precise scope for the investigation and guiding the subsequent analysis.
The key concept of sustainability is explicitly identified, laying a solid foundation for integration throughout the analysis.
Ensure the page header consistently displays the report title or page numbers to improve navigational clarity.
The table of contents is well-structured with clear headings and accurate page references, enhancing the documentâs organization.
The introduction provides useful context on The Body Shop but could be strengthened by explicitly defining sustainability and linking it to supply-chain impacts.
While the methodology section outlines document sources, a clearer rationale for selecting these specific reports would strengthen the justification of evidence.
The introduction is clearly titled and delineated, guiding the reader effectively into the IA.
The analysis section is clearly demarcated with an appropriate heading, aiding structural coherence.
Selecting the Triple Bottom Line reflects a purposeful choice of tool suited to evaluating sustainability across economic, social, and environmental dimensions.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The definition of the Triple Bottom Line is descriptive but lacks immediate linkage to The Body Shopâs specific initiatives in this section.
The discussion of the refill scheme would benefit from quantified data on cost savings and waste reduction to substantiate the stated efficiencies.
Using the CSR Matrix as a materiality assessment tool is appropriate for evaluating stakeholder priorities and ESG issues.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The CSR matrix description remains general, with minimal insight into the specific findings of Natura & Coâs materiality report for The Body Shop.
Correct the formatting of footnotes and mathematical insets to ensure consistency and readability in citations.
The net-zero section effectively incorporates quantitative emission reductions, strengthening the evidence base for environmental impact.
The discussion of net-zero limitations is insightful but would gain depth by linking complications to specific supplier or stakeholder data.
Introducing the stakeholder map tool is effective in framing the analysis of influence and interest among key groups.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Embed axis labels or a legend within the materiality figure to enhance interpretability and ensure the reader understands data dimensions.
The paragraph ends abruptly and the final sentence is incomplete, indicating a need for careful proofreading to ensure clarity.
The conclusion section is clearly labeled, providing a dedicated space to synthesize findings.
The conclusion summarizes key findings but would be strengthened by deeper integration of sustainability theory and explicit linkage to the research question.
The reference list exceeds the recommended range of 3â5 core documents; consider focusing on the most relevant sources to meet rubric criteria.
Including both corporate reports and academic sources demonstrates breadth in supporting documentation and enriches the investigation.
Some citations use inconsistent URL formats; ensure all references adhere to a single, recognized citation style.
Footnote formatting in the introduction appears conflated and should be standardized for clarity and academic convention.
